transcript.text: what about the sort of lovely subjection
Ada Lovelace that Turing writes about
that AI is somehow just super reactive
and it's not generative it doesn't have
an Impulse to create itself you have to
program it lady Loveless objection is
actually one of the nine that touring
addresses in this essay the main issue
there is that lady Loveless wasn't
anticipating adaptive programs because
if you take a system like Lambda one of
the main qualitative differences between
Lambda and gpt3 is in the course of a
conversation if you want to talk to
Lambda about something that it doesn't
know a lot about it can go out and read
up on it and actively incorporate
whatever it learned into its language
model still only doing the reading in
order to have a baseline level of
knowledge for a conversation that it's
sort of tasked with having whereas it's
like why does Van Gogh decide to draw a
starry night like where does that come
from
